_metadata.description: Rule-based DFT functional recommendation decision tree with backward-compatible advisory metadata
_metadata.upgrade_notes: Cross-validated two proposal sets. Shipped defaults kept conservative; metadata and missing purpose coverage upgraded; unsupported high-end options remain alternatives only. Patch 3 alignment: patch_2 adopted as the safer baseline; wB97X-D downgraded to caution/future-candidate status pending live PySCF validation; 3d_tm.single_point confidence aligned to the stricter SSE17 reading.

default.rationale: B3LYP-D3(BJ)/def2-SVP is a robust, well-benchmarked combination for small organic molecules. B3LYP is the most widely validated hybrid functional with 20% Hartree-Fock exchange. The D3(BJ) dispersion correction (Grimme 2011) eliminates the known over-repulsiveness of uncorrected B3LYP. def2-SVP provides a balanced double-zeta description with polarization functions on all atoms. GMTKN55 WTMAD-2 for B3LYP-D3(BJ) is 6.42 kcal/mol.
default.applicability: general-purpose baseline for neutral closed-shell small organic molecules.
default.community_consensus: Strong consensus for geometry-first B3LYP/PBE0-style workflows.

geometry_opt.rationale: For geometry optimization of small organic molecules, B3LYP-D3(BJ)/def2-SVP provides an excellent cost-accuracy balance. Bond length MAE is typically 0.008 Angstrom. The double-zeta basis is sufficient for geometry, though single-point energies should use a larger basis.

default.rationale: B3LYP remains the most widely used and validated functional for 3d transition metal complexes. The 20% HF exchange provides a reasonable balance for geometry optimization. However, spin-state energetics are highly sensitive to the HF exchange fraction. ECPs for elements beyond Kr are built into the def2 basis sets. SSE17 (10.1039/D4SC05471G) shows that legacy TM defaults such as B3LYP and TPSSh are not universal spin-state choices; this shipped rule should be treated as a pragmatic baseline rather than a final energetic authority.
default.applicability: general-purpose baseline for mononuclear first-row transition-metal complexes.
default.community_consensus: Recent benchmarking weakens claims of universality for legacy TM defaults.

default.rationale: Unrestricted B3LYP (UB3LYP) is the standard approach for radical species. Spin contamination should always be monitored via the <S^2> expectation value. If <S^2> deviates more than 10% from the expected value, the results may be unreliable. Recent radical-focused benchmarking supports M06-2X and range-separated cross-checks, so UB3LYP is kept as a stable baseline rather than a one-method answer.

esp_mapping.rationale: Unrestricted B3LYP (UB3LYP) is the standard approach for radical species. Spin contamination should always be monitored via the <S^2> expectation value. If <S^2> deviates more than 10% from the expected value, the results may be unreliable. Use qualitatively and re-check spin contamination before drawing strong electrostatic conclusions.

avoid_when: quantitative charge fitting without an upgraded basis

default.rationale: Charged species require a larger basis set (at least triple-zeta) to adequately describe the diffuse electron density of anions or the compact density of cations. For anions, consider adding diffuse functions (def2-TZVPD). Dispersion correction is essential for ion-pair interaction energies. Range-separated hybrids such as wB97X-D remain preferred cross-checks for long-range self-interaction-sensitive cases.
default.applicability: general-purpose baseline for organic cations, anions, and ion-pair-like systems.
avoid_when: hard anions without diffuse basis support
default.community_consensus: Strong consensus on triple-zeta minimum and long-range checks.

bonding_analysis.rationale: For bonding analysis of charged organic systems, B3LYP-D3(BJ)/def2-TZVP is kept as the conservative default because the larger triple-zeta basis reduces density distortion for cationic or anionic states while remaining compatible with the current PySCF/QCViz runtime. Diffuse-augmented checks should be considered for highly delocalized anions.
bonding_analysis.applicability: Charged main-group molecules where IBO/IAO-style bonding analysis is desired after SCF convergence.
bonding_analysis.avoid_when: Highly diffuse anions, strong charge-transfer states, or clear multireference character.
bonding_analysis.community_consensus: Triple-zeta is a safer floor for charged species; diffuse augmentation may still be needed.
bonding_analysis.implementation_notes: Keep the production default on def2-TZVP. Escalate manually to diffuse basis sets when the density is visibly over-delocalized.
bonding_analysis.basis_upgrade_path: def2-TZVPP or diffuse-augmented basis in a manual expert workflow.
bonding_analysis.validation_notes: Check orbital localization stability against a diffuse-basis single-point for anions.
